ddls:
CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being granted permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., view_performance_data, edit_test_cases) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the system configuration */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the system configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_messages" (
  "message_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system message */,
  "message_text" TEXT /* Text of the system message */,
  "message_type" TEXT /* Type of the system message (e.g., error, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_id")
)
column_names: ID of the user being granted permission
Name of the permission (e.g., view_performance_data, edit_test_cases)
Unique identifier for each system configuration
Unique identifier for each system message
Type of the system message (e.g., error, warning, info)
